=== plugins.py — PluginContext class ===
289:class PluginContext:
290-    """Facade given to plugins so they can register tools and hooks."""
291-
292-    def __init__(self, manifest: PluginManifest, manager: "PluginManager"):
293-        self.manifest = manifest
294-        self._manager = manager
295-        # Lazy-built host-owned LLM facade — see ctx.llm property below.
296-        self._llm: Any = None
297-
298-    # -- host-owned LLM access ----------------------------------------------
299-
300-    @property
301-    def llm(self) -> Any:
302-        """Return the plugin's :class:`agent.plugin_llm.PluginLlm` facade.
303-
304-        Lets trusted plugins run host-owned chat or structured completions
305-        against the user's active model and auth without bringing their
306-        own provider keys. Override capability (model, agent id, auth
307-        profile) is fail-closed by default and gated through
308-        ``plugins.entries.<plugin_id>.llm.*`` config keys.
309-
310-        See :mod:`agent.plugin_llm` for the full surface."""
311-        if self._llm is None:
312-            from agent.plugin_llm import PluginLlm
313-            plugin_id = self.manifest.key or self.manifest.name
314-            self._llm = PluginLlm(plugin_id=plugin_id)
315-        return self._llm
316-
317-    # -- tool registration --------------------------------------------------
318-
319-    def register_tool(
320-        self,
321-        name: str,
322-        toolset: str,
323-        schema: dict,
324-        handler: Callable,
325-        check_fn: Callable | None = None,
326-        requires_env: list | None = None,
327-        is_async: bool = False,
328-        description: str = "",
329-        emoji: str = "",
330-        override: bool = False,
331-    ) -> None:
332-        """Register a tool in the global registry **and** track it as plugin-provided.
333-
334-        Pass ``override=True`` to replace an existing built-in tool with the
335-        same name (e.g. swap the default ``browser_navigate`` for a custom
336-        CDP-backed implementation). Without it, attempting to register a name
337-        already claimed by a different toolset is rejected.
338-        """
339-        from tools.registry import registry
340-
341-        registry.register(
342-            name=name,
343-            toolset=toolset,
344-            schema=schema,
345-            handler=handler,
346-            check_fn=check_fn,
347-            requires_env=requires_env,
348-            is_async=is_async,
349-            description=description,
350-            emoji=emoji,
351-            override=override,
352-        )
353-        self._manager._plugin_tool_names.add(name)
354-        logger.debug(
355-            "Plugin %s registered tool: %s%s",
356-            self.manifest.name, name, " (override)" if override else "",
357-        )
358-
359-    # -- message injection --------------------------------------------------
360-
361-    def inject_message(self, content: str, role: str = "user") -> bool:
362-        """Inject a message into the active conversation.
363-
364-        If the agent is idle (waiting for user input), this starts a new turn.
365-        If the agent is running, this interrupts and injects the message.
366-
367-        This enables plugins (e.g. remote control viewers, messaging bridges)
368-        to send messages into the conversation from external sources.
369-

=== plugins.py — register_hook def ===
936-        )
937-
938:    def register_hook(self, hook_name: str, callback: Callable) -> None:
939-        """Register a lifecycle hook callback.
940-
941-        Unknown hook names produce a warning but are still stored so
942-        forward-compatible plugins don't break.
943-        """
944-        if hook_name not in VALID_HOOKS:
945-            logger.warning(
946-                "Plugin '%s' registered unknown hook '%s' "
947-                "(valid: %s)",
948-                self.manifest.name,
949-                hook_name,
950-                ", ".join(sorted(VALID_HOOKS)),
951-            )
952-        self._manager._hooks.setdefault(hook_name, []).append(callback)
953-        logger.debug("Plugin %s registered hook: %s", self.manifest.name, hook_name)
954-
955-    # -- skill registration -------------------------------------------------
956-
957-    def register_skill(
958-        self,
959-        name: str,
960-        path: Path,
961-        description: str = "",
962-    ) -> None:
963-        """Register a read-only skill provided by this plugin.
964-
965-        The skill becomes resolvable as ``'<plugin_name>:<name>'`` via
966-        ``skill_view()``.  It does **not** enter the flat
967-        ``~/.hermes/skills/`` tree and is **not** listed in the system
968-        prompt's ``<available_skills>`` index — plugin skills are

=== plugins.py — invoke_hook def ===
1535-    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
1536-
1537:    def invoke_hook(self, hook_name: str, **kwargs: Any) -> List[Any]:
1538-        """Call all registered callbacks for *hook_name*.
1539-
1540-        Each callback is wrapped in its own try/except so a misbehaving
1541-        plugin cannot break the core agent loop.
1542-
1543-        Returns a list of non-``None`` return values from callbacks.
1544-
1545-        For ``pre_llm_call``, callbacks may return a dict describing
1546-        context to inject into the current turn's user message::
1547-
1548-            {"context": "recalled text..."}
1549-            "recalled text..."          # plain string, equivalent
1550-
1551-        Context is ALWAYS injected into the user message, never the
1552-        system prompt.  This preserves the prompt cache prefix — the
1553-        system prompt stays identical across turns so cached tokens
1554-        are reused.  All injected context is ephemeral — never
1555-        persisted to session DB.
1556-        """
1557-        kwargs.setdefault("telemetry_schema_version", OBSERVER_SCHEMA_VERSION)
1558-        callbacks = self._hooks.get(hook_name, [])
1559-        results: List[Any] = []
1560-        for cb in callbacks:
1561-            try:
1562-                ret = cb(**kwargs)
1563-                if ret is not None:
1564-                    results.append(ret)
1565-            except Exception as exc:
1566-                logger.warning(
1567-                    "Hook '%s' callback %s raised: %s",
--
1648-
1649-
1650:def invoke_hook(hook_name: str, **kwargs: Any) -> List[Any]:
1651-    """Invoke a lifecycle hook on all loaded plugins.
1652-
1653-    Returns a list of non-``None`` return values from plugin callbacks.
1654-    """
1655-    return get_plugin_manager().invoke_hook(hook_name, **kwargs)
1656-
1657-
1658-def has_hook(hook_name: str) -> bool:
1659-    """Return True when a hook has registered callbacks."""
1660-    return get_plugin_manager().has_hook(hook_name)

1663-_thread_tool_whitelist = threading.local()

1666-def set_thread_tool_whitelist(
1667-    allowed: Optional[Set[str]],
1668-    deny_msg_fmt: str = "Tool '{tool_name}' denied: not in this thread's tool whitelist",
1669-) -> None:
1670-    _thread_tool_whitelist.allowed = allowed
1671-    _thread_tool_whitelist.fmt = deny_msg_fmt
1672-
1673-
1674-def clear_thread_tool_whitelist() -> None:
1675-    _thread_tool_whitelist.allowed = None
